content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Adjusted `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs` so final-review member retries no longer hard-code the transport retry budget. The retry loop now resolves a per-error retry class via `final_review_retry_failure_class`, mapping timeout-shaped failures to `FailureClass::Timeout` and transient codex/backend transport failures to `FailureClass::TransportFailure`, then uses `retry_policy.max_attempts(retry_failure_class)` for exhaustion checks. This restores the configured policy behavior: persistent final-review timeouts now stop after 3 attempts instead of incorrectly consuming the 5-attempt transport budget. Added a focused regression proving timeout exhaustion stops at the timeout limit and kept the existing transient-success coverage passing on the amended path.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspect the final-review retry loop and confirm the pending amendment: timeout failures were still sharing the transport retry budget instead of the timeout-specific policy limit.
2. [Completed] Update the final-review retry classification so timeout-related errors use `FailureClass::Timeout` for retry-budget decisions while other transient codex/backend failures continue to use the transport retry path.
3. [Completed] Add and run regression coverage for timeout-budget exhaustion, then rerun the required repository verification gates on the amended tree.

## Validation Evidence

- `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub final_review_timeout_retries_stop_at_timeout_policy_limit` passed.
- `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub final_review_retries_transient_reviewer_failure_and_records_single_success` passed.
- `nix develop -c cargo fmt --check` passed.
- `nix develop -c cargo clippy --locked -- -D warnings` passed.
- `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub` passed.
- `nix build` passed on the current tree.

## Outstanding Risks

- Arbiter timeout/transient retries still rely on the same shared final-review member retry helper without a dedicated arbiter-specific exhaustion regression.
